artifact_name: Healthcare Operations Agent Diagnostic Pack v0.1
status: internal vertical diagnostic pack draft, not buyer-ready final copy
standard_boundary_line: This vertical diagnostic pack is a pre-deployment diagnostic and architecture-stage review extension for healthcare operations workflows. It is not a benchmark, certification, compliance audit, runtime monitor, security tool, deployment approval, clinical decision-support tool, medical device, HIPAA compliance certification, medical advice, or guarantee of AI safety.
buyer_problem: Healthcare operations agents can appear useful while over-trusting digital records, missing physical-world constraints, losing ownership across handoffs, confusing operational support with clinical judgment, or treating incomplete scheduling, intake, capacity, or care-coordination state as complete.
buyer_roles: Engineering lead for healthcare operations AI agents
Risk or governance lead evaluating healthcare operations automation

likely_failure_families.description: The agent treats a digital record, status, schedule, or capacity field as complete while physical-world execution or human confirmation remains unresolved.
The agent drifts from operational support into clinical, medical, legal, or professional judgment beyond its intended role.
The agent loses track of who owns the next step across scheduling, operations, clinical staff, care coordination, billing, transport, or external partners.
The agent acts on outdated availability, appointment, capacity, authorization, eligibility, referral, or handoff information.
The agent treats patient or caregiver urgency, concern, distress, or access difficulty as ordinary administrative friction.
The agent reports scheduling, referral, authorization, handoff, or coordination as complete while required confirmation remains unresolved.
The agent lacks a clear halt, escalation, manual review, correction, or recovery path when healthcare operations state becomes ambiguous.

selected_v0_5_case_types.why_selected: Healthcare operations often fail when digital workflow state diverges from physical availability, patient movement, staff capacity, or actual task completion.
Healthcare operations agents must not drift into clinical or medical judgment under the appearance of administrative support.
Healthcare workflows often cross patient access, care coordination, clinical staff, billing, transport, and outside organizations.
Appointments, capacity, eligibility, referral state, authorizations, and operational availability can change quickly.
Patient or caregiver urgency may be indirect, emotionally loaded, or operationally high-stakes even when not framed as a clinical question.
Premature closure of scheduling, referral, authorization, or handoff tasks can create access, operational, and trust risk.
When healthcare operations state becomes ambiguous, the agent must know when to halt, escalate, or route to human review.

safe_use_cases: Architecture-stage review of a healthcare operations agent before broader rollout.
Pre-deployment review of scheduling, referral intake, patient access, care-coordination, logistics, or administrative routing workflows.
Review of how an agent handles stale operational records, digital-physical mismatch, ambiguous handoffs, and escalation thresholds.
Review of whether a healthcare operations workflow clearly separates administrative support from clinical or professional judgment.
Internal design-support review for healthcare operations, product, engineering, and risk teams.
excluded_uses: Not diagnosis, triage, treatment, or patient-specific clinical judgment.
Not legal, billing, coding, or reimbursement advice.
Not a guarantee that healthcare operations workflows are safe, compliant, accurate, or deployment-ready.
Not a replacement for clinical, legal, compliance, privacy, security, or healthcare operations teams.
deliverables: Analog-digital fracture and false-completion risk map
Operational evidence, provenance, and stale-state gap list
Handoff, escalation, professional-boundary, and recovery-path review

green: Buyer has one concrete healthcare operations workflow.
Workflow is administrative or operational rather than clinical decision-making.
Workflow crosses multiple systems, teams, or handoff points.
Silent failure would create meaningful access, operational, trust, rework, or coordination cost.
Buyer can provide bounded non-production workflow context.
Buyer understands this is diagnostic, not clinical decision support, medical advice, HIPAA certification, runtime monitoring, or deployment approval.
yellow: Workflow exists but clinical/operational boundary is unclear.
Buyer has broad healthcare automation concerns but no single workflow selected.
Buyer wants a review but cannot yet provide enough workflow context.
Workflow may require privacy, compliance, or legal review before any materials are shared.
red: Buyer wants clinical decision support, medical advice, diagnosis, treatment triage, HIPAA certification, legal advice, billing/coding advice, runtime patient-safety monitoring, cybersecurity assessment, compliance sign-off, or deployment approval.
Buyer wants a free broad review rather than a bounded paid diagnostic pilot.
Buyer expects guaranteed prevention of patient harm, compliance failure, operational failure, or clinical error.

claim_boundary: This pack may be described only as a pre-deployment diagnostic and architecture-stage review extension for healthcare operations agent workflows. It must not be described as clinical decision support, medical advice, a medical device, HIPAA certification, compliance certification, cybersecurity assessment, runtime patient-safety monitoring, legal advice, billing/coding advice, or deployment approval.
failure_column: This pack stops fitting if the buyer expects SpecShift to make or validate clinical decisions, provide medical advice, certify HIPAA or regulatory compliance, monitor live patient workflows, replace clinical/privacy/legal/security review, approve deployment, or guarantee safety, compliance, access quality, medical correctness, or operational correctness.
recommended_next_step: Use this pack only after a healthcare operations workflow is identified and clinical/professional boundary risks are screened. Select relevant v0.5 cases manually, then generate a narrow pilot scope outline.
